name: Kailua
desc: Windward Oahu's crown jewel — world-famous beach, walkable town, top schools, Target and Whole Foods, gold standard for family living.
insider: The south end of Kailua Beach near the boat ramp is the least crowded section — locals know to set up there
Buzz's Steakhouse has been here since 1962 — the lanai tables at sunset are magical, get the original steak and lobster
Thursday night is the best time for Kailua town — less traffic, restaurants have specials, and the vibe is mellow
Bellows Beach on MCBH opens to the public on weekends — military-quality beach with camping and a locals-only feel
Moke's Bread & Breakfast serves lilikoi pancakes that rival Boots & Kimo's with a fraction of the wait time
The Kailua bike path from town to the beach is flat and easy — rent a bike and skip the parking nightmare entirely
Lanikai Juice has the best cold-pressed juices and acai bowls in town — the Lanikai Bowl is a local institution
Kailua has the best public schools on the windward side — Kailua High, Kailua Intermediate, and several strong elementaries
President Obama vacationed in Kailua multiple times — the town gained national attention and real estate prices jumped
Kalapawai Market on the way to Lanikai is a gourmet deli hidden in a tiny building — the sandwiches are incredible
Parking at Kailua Beach Park costs nothing but fills up by 9am on weekends — the neighborhood street parking has time limits
The town is walkable enough that many residents go car-free for daily errands — rare for anywhere on Oahu
